product_direction: AI-generated recommendation in -> defensible Trust Decision Record out
doctrine: The workbench exists to produce the record.
Vendor risk is the first wedge.
Buyer-facing routes should lead to the guided vendor-risk demo. Internal QA and workbench routes must not be presented as part of the public demo.

routes.expected_behavior: Direct users to the guided vendor-risk demo first and redirect after 12 seconds.
must_not: surface workbench as the public demo

routes.expected_behavior: Six-step guided vendor-risk trust decision loop with editable steps, reset, regenerate, record generation, print, JSON download, and follow-up capture.
Show GO when the guided vendor-risk record contract passes. Do not surface as part of the buyer demo.
Validate the expected Trust Decision Record contract. Do not surface as part of the buyer demo.
Validate route doctrine and confirm the guided vendor-risk route remains primary.
Capture final controlled-demo readiness decision locally. Do not surface as part of the buyer demo.
Capture local browser defect reports and allow copy/download JSON. No backend submission.
Provide deeper ATDR workbench inspection without replacing or competing with the guided route as primary demo.
Support smoke-readiness review for existing ATDR workbench.
Support advisor-feedback workflow without distracting from the vendor-risk demo.
Preserve prior Executive OS / Trust Kernel experience. Do not delete. Do not restore as root. Do not surface as part of the buyer demo.

non_goals_until_vendor_risk_loop_is_done: Live Google Sheet write without configured endpoint
static_prototype_limitations: Browser Print / Save PDF is current PDF path
CRM capture is local/pending until REPORT_CAPTURE_ENDPOINT is configured
